title: Q1 Results 20th July Live: UltraTech PAT up, IOB, KVB Q1 PAT climb, Mahindra Logistics, Dynamic Cables & Shyam Metalics zoom, Paytm, Sobha, JP Power, Bluestone to announce Q1 results - The HinduBusine
text:
businessman investment consultant analyzing company financial report balance sheet statement working with digital graphs. Concept picture for stock market, office, tax,and project. 3D illustration. istock photo for BL | Photo Credit: iStockphoto

Q1 Results Today, 20th July 2026 Live Updates: Find all the latest Q1 results 2026 updates of UltraTech Cement, Indian Overseas Bank, Authum Investment & Infrastructure, Karur Vysya Bank, Shyam Metalics & Energy, Central Mine Planning & Design Institute, Sobha and Canara HSBC Life Insurance Company.

Bank stocks - HDFC Bank, Axis Bank, Kotak Mahindra Bank, Yes Bank - underperformed following Q1 results;

JSW Steel, Reliance Industries & ICICI Bank rose

UltraTech Cement reports 17% profit rise and plans significant capacity expansion by fiscal 2028 amid strong sales volumes.

Sensex ended 442.93 pts or 0.57% lower at 77,708.52; and Nifty 50 fell 95.80 pts or 0.39% to 24,238.50.

Aurum PropTech reports ₹45 crore net profit in Q1 2026-27, driven by higher revenue and property sales gains.

IOB has set a target of raising around $1 billion in FCNR (B) and Overseas Foreign Currency Borrowings by September, says the MD. It has already raised $300 million in FCNR (B) deposits alone as of date.

STEEL EXCHANGE INDIA’s standalone net profit for the quarter ended June 2026 stood at Rs 15.02 crore compared to Rs 10.22 crore in the same quarter last year.

Total income rose to ₹10,938 crore during the April-June period from ₹8,866.47 crore a year earlier, the bank said in a stock exchange filing

Karur Vysya Bank reported net profit for the quarter ended June 2026 at Rs 755.70 crore (up 44.9%) from Rs 521.45 crore in the same quarter last year.

Shares were down 4% to Rs 298.25 on the NSE.

Consolidated Net Sales rose to Rs. 24,465 crores, compared with Rs. 21,040 crores in the corresponding period of the previous year, registering a growth of 16%.

• Total Credit increased by 22.75 % to ₹3,22,132 crore.

• RAM Segment of the Bank increased by 35.82% YoY, within which 36.49% growth in Retail, 46.84% growth in Agri and 16.15% growth in MSME

• Net Profit increased by 49.32 % to ₹ 1,659 crore.

• Operating profit increased by 14.21 % to ₹ 2,693 crore.

• Net Interest Income (NII) increased by 34.30 % to ₹ 3,688 crore.

• Domestic NIM increased to 3.48 % while Global NIM increased to 3.37 %

• Return on Assets (ROA) increased to 1.41 %

• Return on Equity (ROE) increased to 22.69 %

Shyam Metalics reports a 21% increase in Q1 PAT to ₹351 crore, with revenue rising to ₹5,502 crore.

UltraTech Cement‘s standalone net profit for the quarter ended June 2026 rose 7.4% to Rs 2,397.12 crore as against Rs 2,231.79 crore in the corresponding quarter a year ago.

“Q1 F27 reflects the continued momentum of our transformation journey evolving into a growth journey. Strong revenue growth, a return to healthy profitability, and improved business fundamentals demonstrate the impact of disciplined execution, sharper customer-level economics, and a relentless focus on operational excellence. Performance this quarter was driven by continued strength in our Contract Logistics business with new customer wins across sectors, significant progress in the Express business turnaround, and sustained improvements in operational efficiency across the network. At the same time, our e-commerce and quick commerce business continues to scale, reinforcing our position in fast-growing logistics segments. The foundations we have built over the last year are now translating into stronger business outcomes. We remain focused on profitable growth, intelligent scale, superior customer experience, and our vision of becoming India’s #1 logistics services provider through technology-led solutions and a passionate team.”

- Hemant Sikka, Managing Director and CEO of Mahindra Logistics Ltd

Q1 F27 (Consolidated)  Revenue Rs. 2,003 cr, up 23% YoY and 12% QoQ

 EBITDA Rs. 115 cr, up 51% YoY and 3% QoQ

 PAT improvement continues for the 4th quarter in a row, with reported PAT at Rs. 25.4 cr, vs Rs. (10.8) cr in Q1 F26 and Rs 20.2 cr in Q4 F26

 EPS (Diluted) Rs 2.55 as compared to Rs (1.44) in Q1F26 and Rs 2.03 in Q4F26

Markets off the day’s low: Sensex traded 411.23 pts or 0.53% lower at 77,740.22; and Nifty 50 declined 97.50 pts or 0.40% to 24,236.80.

Mahindra Logistics reported standalone net profit for the quarter ended June 2026 at Rs 29 crore compared to Rs 6.44 crore in the same quarter last year.

Shares jumped 8% to Rs 417.50 on the NSE.

Indian Overseas Bank’s standalone net profit for the quarter ended June 2026 increased 49.3% to Rs 1659.24 crore from Rs 1111.04 crore in the same quarter last year.

Shares surged 3% to Rs 34.95 on the NSE.

Dynamic Cables reported net profit for the quarter ended June 2026 at Rs 24.95 crore (up 37% y-o-y) from Rs 18.20 crore in the same quarter last year.

Shares traded 7% higher at Rs 413.10 on the NSE.

Shyam Metalics and Energy’s consolidated net profit for the quarter ended June 2026 stood at Rs 350.73 crore compared to Rs 290.67 crore in the same quarter last year.

Board approved interim dividend of Rs 1.80 per share. Record date is July 24.

Board approved fund raising of up to Rs 4,500 crore by way of issuance of equity shares, eligible securities, and/or any other equity-linked instruments, including convertible or nonconvertible preference shares, or fully/partly convertible debentures.

Shares zoomed 6% to Rs 1,082 on the NSE.

Turtlemint shares rise 13% after reporting first profitable quarter and 57% revenue growth for FY26.

JK Cement shares traded 2% higher at Rs 5,525 on the NSE, hitting a high of Rs 5,544.

Consolidated profit declined over 15% to Rs 274.62 crore in Q1FY27.

Swaraj Engines reported net profit for the quarter ended June 2026 at Rs 55.53 crore compared to Rs 49.97 crore in the same quarter last year.

Tata Technologies stock declined 2% to Rs 742.65 on the NSE at 10.52 am, after soaring to a high of Rs 775 from the previo
